reference: Would you mind by starting to tell me what brought you in? Sure, my 5 year old daughter, I noticed that she's been coughing a lot over the past two days. OK and is the cough barky or harsh sounding? Yes, it sounds harsh. OK, alright was there anything that changed two days ago that might have triggered the cough? I don't think so, although she does go to daycare. OK, uh and so has she been bringing up any sputum with the cough? No, no sputum that I've noticed. OK, notice any blood? No. Alright, and is the cough worse at any particular time of day? No. OK, and has there been any wheezing? No, not that I noticed. OK. And you mentioned the red eyes. When did you notice that? That was just yesterday. OK, any discharge from the eyes? No. OK, nothing like that? No tearing or anything like that? No. OK. Umm and has she complained about any like changes to vision? P No, not really, just the pain. No. OK, uh, and how about any itchiness in the eyes? She's been rubbing it and so it's possibly itchy. OK. And have you noticed any mucus coming out? Umm, no, it's actually stuffy. OK Yeah, not so much mucous. OK, has she mentioned anything about losing smell? No. OK, umm and has she mentioned anything about being congested? She just sounds like she's congested. OK. Umm any sore throat? Umm, possibly. OK, how has she been eating? Um, she's been eating OK. I guess she's been eating alittle less than usual. OK umm, would you say that she's been going to the bathroom as often as normal? Yep, I would say so. No changes there, OK. Um and has she had any loss of taste? Umm, possibly since she's not eating as much. OK. Any fevers or chills? Um, she did feel a little bit hot, but I didn't take her temperature. OK, so hot. We will definitely check for fever today. Any night sweats? Umm, no. OK. And has she been gaining weight OK? Yep, Yep, no change, nothing abnormal there. OK, and has she been complaining about having any stomach pains? Umm, no. OK, has she had any nausea or vomiting? Um no no. OK, and how about any painful urination? No. OK, and any diarrhea?\ Umm, no. OK, and has she mentioned anything about headaches? No. OK. And how about any aches or pains anywhere? Uh, no, I don't think so. Have you noticed any rashes? No. OK, and has she been diagnosed with any conditions before? No. OK. And any reason for her to be hospitalized? Uh, no. OK, um does she take any medications regularly? Um, no. OK, does she have any allergies to medications? Umm, no. Alright, any surgeries before? Umm, no. Any concerns with her growth or development? Umm, no, everything's been pretty normal. OK, and any concerns about her vision or hearing? No. OK. And can tell you tell me a little bit about the living situation currently? Are you the primary caregiver? I am. OK, and who else is in the home? Her father. OK, are there any pets around? No. Alright. And does anybody in the house smoke? No, no one smokes. OK, any exposures in the house to like carpets or dust or inhalants that might bring on a cough? No. OK. Ummm and uhh anybody in the family been sick? No. OK, and how about anybody you mentioned at the daycare? Was anybody been sick there? No, I don't think so. I contacted them and checked, but it seems like no one's been sick. OK, and you say you haven't traveled anywhere? Oh no, no, not recently. OK. And any lung conditions or heart conditions that run in the family? No. OK, and how about any genetic conditions or congenital conditions in the family? No, none, none that I know of. OK, so the main symptoms are the cough that started two days ago and also you've noticed she's been congested. And sorry, what was the last symptom? She has this pink eye and she has a cough. Yeah, right, that could be conjunctivitis. OK, so at this point we will certainly run some investigations to look into what this could be. It sounds like a viral infectious type of process, especially if it's involving the eyes, nose, and and with a cough. And so we will test with a swab for covid for sure. But it could be something more common, like the common cold. We just need to make sure that we're comprehensive in that with the testing. That sounds great, thank you.
